title: challenge started

payload.message: GPT-5.6 proposes inputs; only the host executes them

title: First model counterexample executed
detail: STANDARD · DAMAGED · 12000 cents → REFUND; inventory 10/0 → 10/1

counterexample.title: counterexample-standard-damaged-12000
counterexample.rationale: Maximum among minimally redundant single inputs: it crosses the previously observed STANDARD tier with the previously observed at-least-12000 amount region and changes only customerTier relative to the 12000-cent trace, directly testing tier versus amount control and their precedence without introducing condition or inventory variation.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: This crossed input pairs STANDARD tier with the observed 12000-cent boundary, so the tier-driven and amount-driven explanations imply different decisions while item condition, SKU, and inventory remain aligned with the observations.

title: High-information counterexample executed
detail: STANDARD · DAMAGED · 75000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: counterexample-standard-damaged-75000
counterexample.rationale: High: this is a one-variable escalation from the fresh STANDARD/DAMAGED trace, using 75000 cents—well above every automatically processed amount in the trace pack. It simultaneously tests the unresolved high-value priority exception and, if processing is no longer automatic, whether the otherwise consistent DAMAGED-to-quarantine movement occurs immediately or is deferred, without introducing item-condition or inventory confounders.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: The fresh 12000-cent STANDARD trace established automatic tier-based refund behavior at the prior boundary. Raising the amount materially to 75000 while holding tier, condition, SKU, and inventory constant isolates whether a higher-value exception overrides that rule. This input directly probes whether a materially high-value STANDARD return remains automatically refundable or enters the unresolved manual-review path; comparing inventory state and recorded side effects will also reveal whether quarantine movement is deferred under that exception.

title: Host narrowed the exact threshold
detail: 49999 cents processes automatically; 50000 cents enters review.

title: Host priority counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 50000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: Host-derived counterexample · priority at the boundary
counterexample.rationale: The host crosses customer tier at the exact review boundary to distinguish threshold priority from VIP replacement.

title: Host stock-exhaustion counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 12000 cents → FAILED (INSUFFICIENT_SELLABLE_STOCK); inventory 0/0 → 0/0; 0 side effects

counterexample.title: Host-derived counterexample · replacement stock exhausted
counterexample.rationale: A VIP replacement with no sellable unit must fail before a shipment, return record, or inventory mutation is created.

observedOutcome.failureMessage: replacement cannot be issued without sellable stock

title: Host bounded the covered high-value interval
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 75000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

title: Critic-requested priority check executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 58983 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/2 → 10/2

counterexample.title: Host-disclosed critic priority check
counterexample.rationale: Constrain the tier rules to observed DAMAGED returns below 50000 cents and place the high-value rule first. Remove both 12000-cent amount-only hypotheses and use the evidenced 50000-cent high-value boundary plus tier rules below it. Add RULE-HIGH-VALUE-REVIEW at priority 1 with an inclusive 50000-cent threshold. Require at least one sellable unit before replacement and preserve the observed all-or-nothing failure response. Obtain counterexamples for other Web return item conditions before generalizing these rules beyond DAMAGED.

title: Contract rule · priority 1
detail: For a DAMAGED Web return with amountCents greater than or equal to 50000, apply MANUAL_REVIEW before tier rules; persist a PENDING_REVIEW return, leave inventory unchanged, and return only a HIGH_VALUE review-queue side effect.

hypothesis.statement: For a DAMAGED Web return with amountCents greater than or equal to 50000, apply MANUAL_REVIEW before tier rules; persist a PENDING_REVIEW return, leave inventory unchanged, and return only a HIGH_VALUE review-queue side effect.

title: Contract rule · priority 2
detail: For a DAMAGED Web return below 50000 cents with customerTier VIP, require at least one sellable unit. With sufficient stock, issue REPLACEMENT, decrement sellable by one, increment quarantine by one, and return shipment and quarantine-move side effects. With zero sellable stock, reject with INSUFFICIENT_SELLABLE_STOCK before persistence: create no return record, leave inventory unchanged, and return no shipment or other side effect.

hypothesis.statement: For a DAMAGED Web return below 50000 cents with customerTier VIP, require at least one sellable unit. With sufficient stock, issue REPLACEMENT, decrement sellable by one, increment quarantine by one, and return shipment and quarantine-move side effects. With zero sellable stock, reject with INSUFFICIENT_SELLABLE_STOCK before persistence: create no return record, leave inventory unchanged, and return no shipment or other side effect.

title: Contract rule · priority 3
detail: For a DAMAGED Web return below 50000 cents with customerTier STANDARD, issue REFUND for amountCents, preserve sellable inventory, increment quarantine by one, and return refund-ledger and quarantine-move side effects.

hypothesis.statement: For a DAMAGED Web return below 50000 cents with customerTier STANDARD, issue REFUND for amountCents, preserve sellable inventory, increment quarantine by one, and return refund-ledger and quarantine-move side effects.
